---

## Figure Type

This is an **administrative index table** (список графических приложений — "List of Graphic Appendices to the Report"), not a geological illustration itself. It is a catalogue/inventory page listing the graphic attachments included in Volume V of the report, formatted as a structured table with seven columns.

---

## Coverage and Subject

The table covers **Volume V (Том V)** of the report and lists **geological cross-sections (геологический разрез)** drawn at a scale of **1:200** for individual boreholes. The boreholes listed are numbered sequentially and include:

- **№ 142, 143, 144, 146, 147, 148, 150, 151, 152, 153, 154, 155, 156, 157, 158, 159, 161, 162**

This covers entries **1 through 18** in the appendix list (sequential numbers in the left column). Note that borehole № 145, 149, and 160 appear to be absent from this sequence, possibly indicating they were either not drilled, not logged graphically, or listed elsewhere.

---

## Key Content

The table has **seven columns**:

| Col | Heading | Content |
|-----|---------|---------|
| 1 | №№ п/п | Sequential entry number (1–18 on this page) |
| 2 | №№ чертежей | Drawing/sheet numbers (63–81, corresponding to the physical drawing numbers in the archive) |
| 3 | Наименования графических приложений | Title of each graphic appendix |
| 4 | Масштаб | Scale |
| 5 | К-во листов | Number of sheets |
| 6 | Степень секретности | Security classification |
| 7 | Вид бумаги | Paper type |

**Key observations:**
- All 18 entries are **геологический разрез по скважине №XXX** ("Geological cross-section for borehole No. XXX")
- All are drawn at **scale 1:200** (масштаб 1:200)
- Each drawing occupies **1 sheet** (к-во листов = I)
- All are classified as **не секретно** ("not secret / unclassified")
- All are printed on **светочувствительная** paper ("photosensitive/blueprint paper," i.e., diazo/blueprint copies)
- The drawings are numbered **63 through 81** in the overall drawing register, with drawing number 66 apparently absent (the sequence jumps from 65 to 67, corresponding to the gap between boreholes 144 and 146)
- Checkmarks (√ or V) appear in the left margin next to each entry, likely indicating that each drawing has been verified as present in the archive

## Research Value

This table serves as a **finding aid and archival inventory** for Volume V of the report, confirming that 18 individual borehole geological cross-sections exist at 1:200 scale for boreholes 142–162 (with several numbers skipped). For a geologist or AI research assistant, it establishes **which boreholes were logged and drawn up in detail**, enabling targeted retrieval of specific borehole cross-sections relevant to understanding lithological sequences, depth to basement, aquifer geometry, or ore-bearing horizons at this northern Kazakhstan site. The gaps in borehole numbering (missing 145, 149, 160) are potentially significant and warrant investigation into whether those holes were abandoned, dry, or documented elsewhere in the report series.
